Lumi for Mobile App

The highest-CPM ad format in adtech, packaged for your AI app.

iOS, Android, React Native. Native sound (no browser autoplay restriction), full-screen interstitials, and rewarded video — the opt-in format that AppLovin and Unity print money on ($25–50 CPM). 10 placements. Brand budgets you can't get on the web.

Start as a publisher →
See it appear

Your AI replies first.
The rewarded video or sponsored card lands next.

Same campaign renders natively on iOS, Android, and React Native — full-screen interstitial, native banner, or rewarded video. Sound on by default.

A FluentAI iOS preview shown here. The same Lumi for Mobile App SDK ships native iOS, Android, and React Native bindings — full-screen interstitial, rewarded video, native banner, and 7 more placements.
Placements you can serve

10 placements, headlined by rewarded video.

Sponsored citation$6.00 RPM
Suggested chip$4.50 RPM
Inline card$7.50 RPM
Loading-state ad$4.50 RPM
Pre-roll video (sound on)$20.00 RPM
Rewarded video (opt-in)$35.00 RPM
Bottom banner$3.00 RPM
Inline native banner$5.50 RPM
Full-screen interstitial$14.00 RPM
Splash sponsor$11.00 RPM
How it works

From npm install to first impression in ten minutes.

1

Install the SDK

Add @boostbossai/lumi-mobile to your React Native, native iOS (Swift), or Android (Kotlin) project.

2

Mount placements

Pick from 10 placement types. Rewarded video earns the highest CPM in the network — wire it to your earn-credit flow.

3

Earn per impression

Brand budgets flow into mobile creatives with sound on by default. You earn on every impression, every click, every rewarded completion.

Integration snippet

A few lines, your React Native screen.

// In your React Native screen — Lumi for Mobile App:
import { Lumi, RewardedVideo } from '@boostbossai/lumi-mobile';

Lumi.init({ publisherId: 'pub_abc' });

// Drop a rewarded video — the highest-CPM placement in adtech:
<RewardedVideo
  placement="earn-credits"
  onReward={(amount) => grantCredits(amount)}
/>

Same SDK for native iOS (Swift) and Android (Kotlin) — install via CocoaPods or Gradle, same publisher ID, same dashboard. Full docs →

Same SDK, three placement types: rewarded video on iOS, full-screen interstitial on Android, inline native banner on React Native. @boostbossai/lumi-mobile handles the platform-specific rendering.
Who this is for

If your AI ships in the App Store or Play Store, this is your path.

  • ✓You shipped an iOS, Android, or React Native AI app.
  • ✓Examples: AI chat apps, image generators, translation apps, study tools, productivity AI, AI camera apps.
  • ✓You want access to brand budgets that don't show up in browser ad networks — rewarded video, mobile interstitials, native banners.
  • ✓You're NOT shipping a Slack / Discord / Telegram bot — that's a different surface. Lumi for Mobile App is for native mobile apps your users download.
Revenue example

What 20,000 monthly users can earn — and why rewarded video changes the math.

Illustrative — not a guarantee
Monthly active users20,000
Daily active users5,000
Banner impressions / DAU / day~6
Rewarded video completions / DAU / day~0.6
Blended eCPM (rewarded video pulls average up)$8
Monthly publisher revenue~$7,800

One rewarded video impression at $35 CPM equals roughly 10 banner impressions at $3 — that's why mobile AI apps that wire rewarded into their earn-credit loop clear the highest revenue per user in the Lumi network. Numbers are illustrative; actual revenue depends on placement mix, opt-in rate on rewarded, fill rate, and geography.

Frequently asked

The questions every mobile dev asks.

What about App Store rules?

Lumi's mobile placements all comply with Apple App Store and Google Play guidelines. No system-UI mimicry, opt-in (never forced) for rewarded video, user-dismissable interstitials, clear "Sponsored" disclosure on every placement. We don't ship anything that gets your app pulled.

Is rewarded video really $25–50 CPM?

Yes. Rewarded video is the highest-CPM format in adtech because the user opts in for value (in-app credits, premium minutes, virtual currency). Brands pay premium for completed views. AppLovin and Unity built billion-dollar businesses on this single format; Lumi for Mobile App brings the same inventory to AI apps.

Can I really play video with sound?

Yes. Native mobile apps aren't subject to browser autoplay rules — pre-roll and rewarded video play with sound on by default. Same SDK pattern as AdMob and AppLovin; if you've shipped mobile video ads before, this will feel familiar.

Does React Native get parity with native iOS/Android?

Yes. @boostbossai/lumi-mobile ships JS bindings for all 10 placements — rewarded video, interstitial, native banner, the works. Under the hood it bridges to the same native iOS/Android SDKs.

What about COPPA / kids apps?

If your app is in the Kids category or COPPA-flagged, Lumi serves contextual-only inventory and disables behavioral targeting at the SDK level. Flag your app in the dashboard and the SDK respects it automatically.

Ready to monetize your AI mobile app?

10 placements. Rewarded video at $35 eCPM. Weekly payouts.
